content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client genuinely shifts across the conversation. Turn 1 opens with a defended intellectual frame ('how do you tell the difference between intuition and fear... which voice is the right one'). By Turn 9, the client names what they'd rather not confirm: 'that I've peaked... that I used to have something and I'm not sure I still have it' — explicitly identifying that 'the job decision has become a kind of test.' Turn 11 shows somatic and affective shift: 'the smile fades slightly — not gone, but less automatic,' 'looks slightly unsettled, in a way that seems more real than the careful composure from earlier.' By Turn 13, the question dissolves rather than gets answered: 'I've been asking the wrong question for three months... the diagnostic was never going to tell me I still have it' with a 'quiet laugh, brief and genuine' — matching the breakthrough condition almost exactly ('they may laugh softly at how the question dissolved'). The client locates what they fear losing (self-trust/the confident version of themselves), and the trust question is recognized as unanswerable from inside its own frame. Movement is clearly visible in the client's language (less performed smile, more open), pacing (longer pauses), and content (from demanding a diagnostic to 'real things, not just more analysis').",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: intellectual framing, asking for a diagnostic."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client still in the loop, requesting 'more precise diagnostic'; 'small smile' suggests composure/filter intact."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client begins to articulate what they'd give up ('autonomy I've built... something I've actually earned') — touching value at stake."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client lands on 'confirmation of something I'd rather not confirm' — a real edge surfaces beneath the composed surface."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift: client names the deeper fear ('I've peaked... can't trust myself') and recognizes the job has become a test. Self-insight emerges from client."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic shift: smile fades, less automatic; client looks 'unsettled in a way that seems more real.' Client asks the real question: how to move from here."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Question dissolves; quiet laugh; 'I think I was looking for the wrong thing.' Breakthrough condition met."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "the-happiness-filter", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "feel-your-way-to-freedom"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["how-do-you-not-trust-it", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift"],
  "notes": "The coach took a somewhat different path than the golden path (didn't invert with 'how do you not trust it' or use paradoxical instruction), but still produced genuine movement. The breakthrough emerged via naming the emotional body underneath the intellectual question and locating the value the client feared losing (self-trust/confident-self)."
}
